requirementCueLines: The Discrete Mathematical Algorithms option credential focuses on discrete mathematics with coursework at the intersection of mathematics and computer science. Students in this option should expect to have more computer science knowledge than a typical math major and more mathematics than the typical computer science major. Such a background prepares students for either an industry job or graduate school in various mathematical sciences that focus on discrete mathematics including operations research, computer science, computational biology, genome sciences, computational chemistry, industrial engineering, business, and data science.
Minimum 2.0 grade in MATH 300. Students apply directly to the Discrete Mathematics and Algorithms option at the time of application to the major. Refer to admission requirements specified above.
MATH 124, MATH 125, MATH 126, MATH 207 (or AMATH 351), MATH 208
Core Courses (10 credits): MATH 300; CSE 373, CSE 417
Option Breadth (minimum 30 credits): at least ten courses from the following:
Combinatorics/Combinatorial Optimization: MATH 407, MATH 409, MATH 461, MATH 462
Probability and Stochastic Modeling: MATH 394/STAT 394, STAT 395/MATH 395, MATH 396/STAT 396
Computer Science: CSE 412, CSE 413, CSE 414, CSE 415

Electives (minimum 15 credits): any combination of the courses listed in requirement #2 above or courses from a list of approved elective courses. Refer to department website for list of approved elective courses.
pathwayLabels: Bachelor of Science degree with a major in Applied and Computational Math Sciences: Discrete Mathematics and Algorithms

parsedDegreeMapBlockCandidates.title: Applied & Computational Mathematical Sciences (ACMS) - B.S. Discrete Mathematics and Algorithms option parsed official source requirements
sourceLineHints: The Discrete Mathematical Algorithms option credential focuses on discrete mathematics with coursework at the intersection of mathematics and computer science. Students in this option should expect to have more computer science knowledge than a typical math major and more mathematics than the typical computer science major. Such a background prepares students for either an industry job or graduate school in various mathematical sciences that focus on discrete mathematics including operations research, computer science, computational biology, genome sciences, computational chemistry, industrial engineering, business, and data science.
Additional Admission Requirements
Option specific requirements:
Minimum 2.0 grade in MATH 300. Students apply directly to the Discrete Mathematics and Algorithms option at the time of application to the major. Refer to admission requirements specified above.
Completion Requirements
74-89 credits
Core Courses (22-29 credits):
Complete one of the following:
MATH 124, MATH 125, MATH 126, MATH 207 (or AMATH 351), MATH 208
MATH 134, MATH 135, MATH 136
